narrative.headline: 2022 Toyota Prius: electrical system leads a small complaint pool
narrative.summary: Owners filed just 23 complaints with NHTSA, a low total. The electrical system is the most reported area at 26%, followed by the service brakes (17%). No fires and no deaths are reported. The brakes and air bags are the only areas tied to crashes (1 and 2), and the electrical system and air bags account for the reported injuries (3 and 1).

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 6 reports (26%), with 3 injuries and no crashes or fires. One complaint describes instrument cluster icons washing out and becoming unreadable in sunlight.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2022 Toyota Prius. The contact stated that on two separate occasions while shifted into reverse, the brake pedal was depressed; however, the vehicle failed to stop. The contact stated that he applied extra pressure and turned off the hybrid power system to stop
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 4 reports (17%), including 1 crash and no injuries. One owner reported the vehicle failing to stop in reverse despite pressing the brake. Worth testing carefully.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 2 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. One complaint references a recall the owner was not notified about.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Only 2 reports (9%) but both involve a crash, and 1 involves an injury. The most serious area by outcome despite the low count.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: 2 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. One owner described the driver's side window spontaneously shattering at highway speed.
